rulings.Case Title: GIAMPAULO BOSCHETTI et al VS. PACIFIC BAY INVESTMENTS, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Stay Arbitration Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 25, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFFs GIAMPAULO BOSCHETTI & PABO, LLC's Motion To Stay Arbitration Proceedings. The motion is denied. A stay under CCP § 916 is not warranted. The current JAMS arbitration proceeding will not "substantially interfere with the appellate court's ability to conduct the appeal." Varian Medical Systems, Inc. v. Delfino (2005) 35 Cal.4th 180, 189-190. The current JAMS arbitration arises out of events that are unrelated to the claims in this case, and that occurred after the notice of appeal was filed. The claims before the arbitrator are therefore not the subject of the appeal. Further, Plaintiff's concern that the arbitration must be stayed because there is a risk of inconsistent rulings as between the trial court and the arbitrator (in the event the Court of Appeal upholds the trial court's order) is too speculative at this stage to warrant a stay and not a basis to do so under CCP §916. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: BH FINANCIAL SERVICES, LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY VS. MICHAEL PINKSTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Reconsideration Of The Response To Request For Admission And To Reverse The Pltfs Motion To Have Matters Deemed Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 25, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANT MICHAEL PINKSTON's Motion For Reconsideration Of The Response To Request For Admission And To Reverse The Plaintiffs Motion To Have Matters Deemed Admitted. Granted, for good cause shown. Because Defendant represents that he did in fact serve responses to Plaintiff's Request for Admission, those requests will not be deemed admitted for all purposes in this litigation. Plaintiff did not file an opposition to this motion. This motion is being heard by Judge Miller because Judge Pro Tem Renne who signed the original order filed May 10, 2013 is not sitting as a Judge Pro Tem until September 5, 2013. rulings.Case Title: AS YOU SOW, A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT PUBLIC BENEFIT VS. I-TEC ELECTRONICS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Responses To Special Interoggs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 25, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF AS YOU SOW, A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT PUBLIC BENEFIT'S Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interoggatories Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the motion. Sanctions of $3,000, payable within 10 days to plaintiff's counsel, are awarded. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: AS YOU SOW, A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT PUBLIC BENEFIT VS. I-TEC ELECTRONICS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Impose Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 25, 2013, Line 8, PLAINTIFF AS YOU SOW's motion To Impose Sanctions. Motion granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff's motion for sanctions is granted pursuant to CCP § 177.5. Defendant violated the Court's January 18, 2013 order to pay Plaintiff $5000 in attorney's fees within 30 days of entry of the order without good cause or substantial justification. The affidavit of David Kraus, attached as Exhibit C to the papers in opposition, is undated. Plaintiff contends that this declaration is a year old. Without more, the Court cannot find on the record now before the Court that there is currently good cause or substantial justification. Defendant is sanctioned $100 to be paid to the Court within 30 days. Plaintiff's motion for sanctions and expenses pursuant to CRC §§ 2.30(b) and (d) is denied. According to Plaintiff, there is a pending order to show cause hearing set in Department 610 where the matter should be addressed. Finally, Plaintiff can seek judgment to enforce a money order under CCP §§ 680.230 and 680.270. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 25, 2013, Line 16, DEFENDANT REDWOOD EMPIRE TITLE COMPANY OF MENDOCINO COUNTY's motion To Transfer Action And Correction Of Error In Motion To Transfer Motion. The motion is continued to July 23, 2013, to give each party an opportunity to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Each party shall provide a courtesy copy of their filings to department 302 by July 8, 2013, with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)
